trace.rationale: Historical data shows that in MLB games, the first inning has a run scored approximately 55% of the time, based on general patterns from pre-cutoff seasons. No specific team or pitcher adjustments are provided, so the base rate is used.
Historical MLB data shows roughly 52% of first innings have at least one run scored, with no specific team or pitcher information to adjust this baseline.

Historical data shows roughly 50-55% chance of a run being scored in the first inning across MLB games, with slight variation based on team strengths. No specific team or pitcher info provided, so baseline estimate.
